text:
CT CHEST, ABDOMEN, AND PELVIS WITH INTRAVENOUS CONTRAST

CLINICAL INDICATION:
Ms. Margaret Okonkwo is a 48-year-old female with newly diagnosed acute myeloid leukemia with myelodysplasia-related changes (AML-MRC), presenting with leukocytosis (WBC 84,000/uL), severe anemia (Hgb 7.1 g/dL), and thrombocytopenia (platelets 28,000/uL). FLT3-ITD mutation confirmed on molecular studies with high allelic ratio. History of antecedent myelodysplastic syndrome. Study obtained for pre-treatment staging evaluation to assess for extramedullary disease, organomegaly, lymphadenopathy, and to establish baseline prior to induction chemotherapy.

TECHNIQUE:
Multidetector CT of the chest, abdomen, and pelvis was performed following the administration of 100 mL of iohexol (Omnipaque 350) intravenous contrast, administered via right antecubital vein. Imaging was acquired in the portal venous phase. Coronal and sagittal reformats were generated and reviewed. No prior cross-sectional imaging available for comparison.

---

FINDINGS:

CHEST:

Lungs and Airways: The lungs are clear bilaterally. No focal consolidation, ground-glass opacity, or pulmonary nodules are identified. The airways are patent to the segmental level. No endobronchial lesions. No peribronchial thickening or tree-in-bud opacity to suggest an active infectious process.

Pleura: No pleural effusions, either free-flowing or loculated. No pleural thickening or calcification. No pneumothorax.

Mediastinum and Hila: The mediastinum is unremarkable in contour. The thymus is involuted and not enlarged. There is no evidence of mediastinal widening. No discrete mediastinal or hilar lymphadenopathy. The largest visualized mediastinal node measures approximately 6 mm in short axis in the right paratracheal region, well within normal limits. No anterior mediastinal mass or soft tissue density.

Heart and Great Vessels: The cardiac silhouette is normal in size. No pericardial effusion. The thoracic aorta is normal in caliber without aneurysmal dilatation or dissection. The pulmonary arteries appear unremarkable in caliber; no filling defect to suggest acute pulmonary embolism on this portal venous phase study, though the study is not optimized for pulmonary embolism evaluation. Coronary calcification: none identified.

Bones (Thoracic): The visualized thoracic spine demonstrates normal alignment and signal. No osteolytic or osteosclerotic lesions of the ribs, sternum, clavicles, scapulae, or thoracic vertebral bodies. No pathologic compression fracture. No periosteal reaction or cortical disruption. Bone density appears appropriate for patient age.

---

ABDOMEN:

Liver: The liver is normal in size with a smooth contour and homogeneous attenuation on both pre- and post-contrast series. Craniocaudal span estimated at 15.3 cm at the midclavicular line, within expected limits. No focal hepatic lesion, mass, or low-attenuation lesion to suggest infiltration, infarction, or metastatic deposit. The hepatic parenchyma enhances uniformly. The intrahepatic biliary system is not dilated. No perihepatic fluid.

Biliary System: The common bile duct is normal in caliber (approximately 4 mm). The gallbladder is present and unremarkable. No cholelithiasis or choledocholithiasis identified. No gallbladder wall thickening or pericholecystic fluid.

Spleen: The spleen is mildly enlarged, measuring approximately 14.2 cm in its greatest craniocaudal dimension (upper limit of normal 12 cm). The splenic parenchyma enhances homogeneously without focal nodule, infarct, or mass. No perisplenic fluid or subcapsular collection. The spleen has a smooth contour without evidence of rupture. This degree of splenomegaly is consistent with the known diagnosis of leukemia and likely reflects prior marrow dysfunction from the underlying myelodysplastic syndrome with or without sequestration and extramedullary hematopoiesis. No discrete splenic chloroma or infiltrative lesion is identified.

Pancreas: The pancreas is normal in size, contour, and attenuation. No ductal dilatation. No peripancreatic stranding or fluid collection. No focal pancreatic mass.

Adrenal Glands: Bilateral adrenal glands are unremarkable. No adrenal nodule or mass. No thickening or hyperplasia.

Kidneys and Ureters: Both kidneys are normal in size and contour with symmetric, brisk corticomedullary enhancement. No hydronephrosis, hydroureter, or obstructing calculus. No renal mass or cyst of concern. No perirenal fluid or stranding. The ureters are not dilated to the extent visualized.

Gastrointestinal Tract: The visualized loops of small bowel are normal in caliber and wall thickness. No pneumatosis intestinalis. The stomach is decompressed and unremarkable. The colon is not dilated. Appendix is visualized and appears normal with no periappendiceal fat stranding. No free intraperitoneal air or abnormal free fluid.

Retroperitoneum and Mesentery: No retroperitoneal or mesenteric lymphadenopathy. The aorta and inferior vena cava are normal in caliber. No retroperitoneal mass, hematoma, or soft tissue density lesion. The mesenteric fat is clear. No omental caking or implants.

Abdominal Lymph Nodes: No enlarged abdominal, retroperitoneal, or celiac axis lymph nodes. The largest identified node in the para-aortic chain measures less than 8 mm in short axis, not meeting criteria for lymphadenopathy by size. No confluent nodal masses.

Bones (Abdominal): The lumbar vertebral bodies demonstrate normal height and alignment. No lytic destruction, sclerotic lesion, or vertebral body fracture. Pelvic bones and proximal femora unremarkable. No evidence of periosteal reaction or cortical breakthrough to suggest granulocytic sarcoma (chloroma) arising in bone.

---

PELVIS:

Bladder: The urinary bladder is well-distended with smooth walls. No bladder mass, filling defect, or wall thickening.

Gynecologic Structures: The uterus is normal in size and configuration. No adnexal mass, ovarian cyst of concern, or pelvic free fluid.

Pelvic Lymph Nodes: No pelvic or inguinal lymphadenopathy. No soft tissue masses within the pelvic sidewalls or presacral space.

Rectum and Sigmoid Colon: The rectum and sigmoid are unremarkable. No rectal wall thickening or perirectal fat stranding.

Bones (Pelvic): The iliac wings, sacrum, coccyx, and proximal femora are visualized without focal osteolytic or osteoblastic abnormality. No pathologic fracture. No soft tissue mass contiguous with the pelvic skeleton to suggest extramedullary leukemic infiltration or chloroma formation.

Muscles and Soft Tissues: No focal soft tissue mass, hematoma, or collection within the pelvic musculature or subcutaneous tissues. No presacral or pelvic sidewall mass.

---

IMPRESSION:

1. Mild splenomegaly with the spleen measuring 14.2 cm in the craniocaudal dimension, without focal splenic lesion or evidence of splenic chloroma. This finding is compatible with the patient's known diagnosis of acute myeloid leukemia arising from myelodysplastic syndrome, and likely reflects a combination of prior and ongoing marrow failure sequelae and possible extramedullary hematopoiesis.

2. No hepatomegaly. The liver parenchyma is homogeneous and enhances normally without focal lesion to suggest leukemic infiltration or extramedullary disease.

3. No mediastinal, hilar, axillary, abdominal, retroperitoneal, pelvic, or inguinal lymphadenopathy identified.

4. No pleural effusions, pericardial effusion, or pulmonary abnormality. Lungs clear bilaterally.

5. No discrete soft tissue mass in any region to suggest chloroma (granulocytic sarcoma) or other form of extramedullary leukemic infiltration.

6. No lytic or blastic osseous lesion in the visualized axial or appendicular skeleton. No periosteal reaction or cortical erosion to suggest bony extramedullary disease.

7. No ascites or free intraperitoneal fluid.

Overall, this staging CT demonstrates mild splenomegaly consistent with underlying leukemia, without evidence of extramedullary disease burden, organomegaly involving the liver, or lymph node involvement. The chest is clear and no cardiac or pericardial abnormality is identified on this study. These findings are useful as a pre-chemotherapy baseline.
